roadside¶

Docstring for roadside.py

Aubrey Moore (aubreymoore2013@gmail.com) Last modified: 2026-02-19

This module provides python functions for building automated detection of coconut rhinoceros beetle damage in digital images.

Functions¶

flip_wkt_origin(→ str)

Transforms a WKT polygon from top-left origin to bottom-left origin.

conv_poly_from_array_to_wkt(→ str)

conv_poly_from_wkt_to_array(→ numpy.array)

check_gpu()

Checks for GPU availability and prints CUDA version and GPU device name if available.

run_sam3_semantic_predictor(input_image_path, text_prompts)

get_data_for_images_table(→ tuple)

Gets data for for a single image for insertion as a record in the 'images' database table.

get_data_for_detections_table(→ pandas.DataFrame)

Gets data for for a single image for insertion as records in the 'detections' database table.

clean_contour(→ numpy.ndarray)

Internal: Removes spikes and jitter.

calculate_efd(→ numpy.ndarray)

Internal: Standard Kuhl and Giardina EFD math.

get_feature_vector(→ numpy.ndarray)

Processes a polygon and returns a normalized 1D feature vector.

normalize(→ numpy.ndarray)

Internal: Rotation, size, and starting-point invariance.

reconstruct(→ tuple[numpy.ndarray, numpy.ndarray])

Reconstructs (x, y) coordinates from a feature vector.

visualize_harmonics(→ None)

Plots original vs reconstructed shapes for various harmonics.

Module Contents¶

roadside.flip_wkt_origin(wkt_string: str, image_height: int) → str¶

Transforms a WKT polygon from top-left origin to bottom-left origin.

Formula: x’ = x y’ = height - y

roadside.conv_poly_from_array_to_wkt(poly: numpy.array) → str¶
roadside.conv_poly_from_wkt_to_array(poly_wkt: str) → numpy.array¶
roadside.check_gpu()¶

Checks for GPU availability and prints CUDA version and GPU device name if available. Returns True if GPU is available, otherwise False.

roadside.run_sam3_semantic_predictor(input_image_path, text_prompts)¶
roadside.get_data_for_images_table(results_cpu, image_path: str) → tuple¶

Gets data for for a single image for insertion as a record in the ‘images’ database table. Returns data as a tuple: (image_width, image_height, timestamp, latitude, longitude) image_width and image_height come from results_cpu timestamp, latitude, longitude come from the EXIF metadata embedded in the image, if it exists.

roadside.get_data_for_detections_table(results_cpu, image_id: int) → pandas.DataFrame¶

Gets data for for a single image for insertion as records in the ‘detections’ database table. Returns data as a pandas dataframe containing columns for image_id, class_id, poly_wkt, x_min, y_min, x_max, y_max, confidence

roadside.clean_contour(contour: numpy.ndarray, sigma: float = 1.0) → numpy.ndarray¶

Internal: Removes spikes and jitter.

roadside.calculate_efd(contour: numpy.ndarray, harmonics: int = 20) → numpy.ndarray¶

Internal: Standard Kuhl and Giardina EFD math.

roadside.get_feature_vector(contour: numpy.ndarray) → numpy.ndarray¶

Processes a polygon and returns a normalized 1D feature vector.

roadside.normalize(coeffs: numpy.ndarray) → numpy.ndarray¶

Internal: Rotation, size, and starting-point invariance.

roadside.reconstruct(feature_vector: numpy.ndarray, num_points: int = 200) → tuple[numpy.ndarray, numpy.ndarray]¶

Reconstructs (x, y) coordinates from a feature vector.

roadside.visualize_harmonics(contour: numpy.ndarray, harmonic_list: list[int] = [1, 3, 10, 50]) → None¶

Plots original vs reconstructed shapes for various harmonics.

sam3-2026-01-31
